title: Reusable Workflows, Composite Actions, Interfaces, and Permission Flow · Changed-Repository Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer reusable workflows, composite actions, interfaces, and permission flow into a new versioned reusable automation contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned reusable automation contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Choose reusable workflow, composite action, JavaScript action, Docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority.
Define workflow_call inputs, secrets, outputs, validation, defaults, required values, and failure semantics as a versioned interface.
Trace caller and called-workflow permissions, secrets inheritance, environment secret boundaries, nested depth, and non-escalation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Reuse Component Selection
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Reuse Component Selection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose reusable workflow, composite action, javascript action, docker action, script, or package based on job boundary, portability, testing, and authority. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Reuse Component Selection, keep Ci Reuse Component Selection connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-shared-automation-evolution-crkrih". Tailor its reusable-automation.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Shared Automation Evolution into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution, keep Ci Shared Automation Evolution connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-shared-automation-evolution-crkrih
  - competency: ci-shared-automation-evolution
    artifact: reusable-automation.yaml
    decision: shared automation evolution
    environment: protected main branch under a merge queue
    fault-injection: change an action tag to a different commit without workflow edits
    retained-proof: OIDC claims provider condition service account IAM and audit entry
    case-key: crkrih
    change: |
      component: reusable-workflow
      workflow-call: typed-inputs-secrets-outputs
      permissions: caller-bounded
      secret-inheritance: explicit-only
      environment-secret: deployment-job-only
      external-ref: full-workflow-sha
      producer-proof: workflow-ref-and-sha
      compatibility: consumer-contract-suite
      deprecation: measured-migration
    verify: validate caller and callee interfaces permissions secrets outputs pins nested depth changed consumer cases and rollback
    failure: Reject wrapper equivalence, implicit secret inheritance, permission escalation, default-branch refs, or centralization as drift elimination.
    repair: Choose the correct boundary, type the interface, preserve caller limits, pin identity, test consumers, canary change, and deprecate visibly.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Shared Automation Evolution structural check is only one gate. versioned reusable automation contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Ci Shared Automation Evolution condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Ci Shared Automation Evolution output and hide the violated versioned reusable automation contract assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Ci Shared Automation Evolution.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ci Shared Automation Evolution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution.
steps.why: Retrieving Ci Shared Automation Evolution through the concrete versioned reusable automation contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution, keep Ci Shared Automation Evolution connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Shared Automation Evolution precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab reusable workflow hides permissions and secrets, nests mutable dependencies, loses typed outputs, and silently changes callers. Design explicit inputs, outputs, authority, versioning, tests, and migration. Competency probe: Evolve shared automation through semantic interface changes, canaries, deprecation, consumer inventory, rollback, telemetry, and migration ownership.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Shared Automation Evolution, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Centralizing a workflow automatically eliminates configuration drift.
